title: Bonnaya ruelloides
intro: Bonnaya ruelloides, also known as Wild Borreria or Ruello, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bonnaya ruelloides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Bonnaya ruelloides is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can also be used topically to treat skin conditions.
taxonomy: Bonnaya ruelloides belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Bonnaya ruelloides is a genus of flowering plants, and it is native to Australia and New Guinea. The species is known to be a climbing plant with small white flowers. It is also known to be a part of the tribe Spermacoceae within the Rubiaceae family.
